devanagari: सूत उवाच । वैयासकेरिति वचः तत्त्वनिश्चयमात्मनः । उपधार्य मतिं कृष्णे औत्तरेयः सतीं व्यधात् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvācavaiyāsakeriti vacaḥ tattvaniścayamātmanaḥupadhārya matiṃ kṛṣṇe auttareyaḥ satīṃ vyadhāt
translation:
Sūta said:
Having heard Śuka’s speech, which led to the definite understanding of the real nature of the Soul, Parīkṣit (the son. of Uttarā) set his virtuous and steady mind on Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: आत्मजायासुतागार पशुद्रविणबन्धुषु । राज्ये चाविकले नित्यं विरूढां ममतां जहौ ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmajāyāsutāgāra paśudraviṇabandhuṣurājye cāvikale nityaṃ virūḍhāṃ mamatāṃ jahau
translation: He renounced attachment which was always sticking (to his mind), to his body, wife, sons, palace, cattle, wealth, kinsmen and kingdom well equipped with the seven constituent elements of the state (enumerated in works on politics).

devanagari: पप्रच्छ चेममेवार्थं यन्मां पृच्छथ सत्तमाः । कृष्णानुभावश्रवणे श्रद्दधानो महामनाः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: papraccha cemamevārthaṃ yanmāṃ pṛcchatha sattamāḥkṛṣṇānubhāvaśravaṇe śraddadhāno mahāmanāḥ
translation: He, a man of noble mind and intense faith in listening to the great deeds of Kṛṣṇa, asked him (Śuka) the same topic which you, the greatest among good persons, ask me.

devanagari: संस्थां विज्ञाय सन्न्यस्य कर्म त्रैवर्गिकं च यत् । वासुदेवे भगवति आत्मभावं दृढं गतः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃsthāṃ vijñāya sannyasya karma traivargikaṃ ca yatvāsudeve bhagavati ātmabhāvaṃ dṛḍhaṃ gataḥ
translation: Having understood the approach of death and having renounced duties pertaining to the three Purusārthas, viz. dharma, artha and kāma, he with great love or devotion for the Lord, solely devoted himself to self-realization in Lord Vāsudeva.

devanagari: राजोवाच । समीचीनं वचो ब्रह्मन् सर्वज्ञस्य तवानघ । तमो विशीर्यते मह्यं हरेः कथयतः कथाम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: rājovācasamīcīnaṃ vaco brahman sarvajñasya tavānaghatamo viśīryate mahyaṃ hareḥ kathayataḥ kathām
translation: Oh sinless Brāhmaṇa! The words of an omniscient person (like you) are quite good (correct). While you tell me the episodes of Hari, the darkness of my ignorance is dispelled.

devanagari: भूय एव विवित्सामि भगवान् आत्ममायया । यथेदं सृजते विश्वं दुर्विभाव्यमधीश्वरैः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūya eva vivitsāmi bhagavān ātmamāyayāyathedaṃ sṛjate viśvaṃ durvibhāvyamadhīśvaraiḥ
translation: I again wish to know how the Lord has created this universe by His Cosmic Power (Māyā)—the universe which is beyond the comprehension of great gods (such as Brahmā).

devanagari: यथा गोपायति विभुः यथा संयच्छते पुनः । यां यां शक्तिमुपाश्रित्य पुरुशक्तिः परः पुमान् । आत्मानं क्रीडयन्क्रीडन् करोति विकरोति च ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā gopāyati vibhuḥ yathā saṃyacchate punaḥyāṃ yāṃ śaktimupāśritya puruśaktiḥ paraḥ pumānātmānaṃ krīḍayankrīḍan karoti vikaroti ca
translation: Also I wish to know by resorting to what powers the Supreme Man of infinite power, creates, protects and destroys (this universe again) and how he sports himself directly and indirectly (through Brahmā and other gods).

devanagari: नूनं भगवतो ब्रह्मन् हरेरद्‍भुतकर्मणः । दुर्विभाव्यमिवाभाति कविभिश्चापि चेष्टितम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: nūnaṃ bhagavato brahman harerad‍bhutakarmaṇaḥdurvibhāvyamivābhāti kavibhiścāpi ceṣṭitam
translation: Oh Brāhmaṇa! Really the actions of Hari of mysterious and wonderful deeds, appear incomprehensible even to very wise people (what of a person like me!).

devanagari: यथा गुणांस्तु प्रकृतेः युगपत् क्रमशोऽपि वा । बिभर्ति भूरिशस्त्वेकः कुर्वन् कर्माणि जन्मभिः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā guṇāṃstu prakṛteḥ yugapat kramaśo'pi vābibharti bhūriśastvekaḥ kurvan karmāṇi janmabhiḥ
translation: Whether it is simultaneously or one after another in succession, that he assumes the different attributes of Prakṛti, in order to do his work through many incarnations.

devanagari: विचिकित्सितमेतन्मे ब्रवीतु भगवान् यथा । शाब्दे ब्रह्मणि निष्णातः परस्मिंश्च भवान्खलु ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: vicikitsitametanme bravītu bhagavān yathāśābde brahmaṇi niṣṇātaḥ parasmiṃśca bhavānkhalu
translation: As your honour is verily well-versed in the Vedic lore and in the knowledge of Parabrahman, Your Worship may kindly explain this doubt to me.

devanagari: सूत उवाच । इत्युपामंत्रितो राज्ञा गुणानुकथने हरेः । हृषीकेशं अनुस्मृत्य प्रतिवक्तुं प्रचक्रमे ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sūta uvācaityupāmaṃtrito rājñā guṇānukathane hareḥhṛṣīkeśaṃ anusmṛtya prativaktuṃ pracakrame
translation:
Sūta said:
Śuka, who was thus solicited by the king, to describe the attributes of Hari, meditated on Kṛṣṇa and began to address.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । नमः परस्मै पुरुषाय भूयसे । सदुद्‍भवस्थाननिरोधलीलया । गृहीतशक्तित्रितयाय देहिनां । अंतर्भवायानुपलक्ष्यवर्त्मने ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācanamaḥ parasmai puruṣāya bhūyasesadud‍bhavasthānanirodhalīlayāgṛhītaśaktitritayāya dehināṃaṃtarbhavāyānupalakṣyavartmane
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Salute to the Perfect Man of infinite power, who by his sport of creating, sustaining and destroying this world, has assumed three powers, viz. Rajas (Brahmā) etc. [or ‘will, wisdom and action which are eternally in him’].

devanagari: भूयो नमः सद्वृजिनच्छिदेऽसतां । असंभवायाखिलसत्त्वमूर्तये । पुंसां पुनः पारमहंस्य आश्रमे । व्यवस्थितानामनुमृग्यदाशुषे ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūyo namaḥ sadvṛjinacchide'satāṃasaṃbhavāyākhilasattvamūrtayepuṃsāṃ punaḥ pāramahaṃsya āśramevyavasthitānāmanumṛgyadāśuṣe
translation: I offer my salutations again and again to him who removes the afflictions of the good, who curbs down the growth of the irreligious, who orders all gods of Sāttvic body[1] to bestow fruits (upon the devotees, and who confers the knowledge of the self upon those who have established themselves in the order of Parama Haṃsas.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
akhila-sattva-mūrtaye: One who is the embodiment of perfect goodness—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: नमो नमस्तेऽस्त्वृषभाय सात्वतां । विदूरकाष्ठाय मुहुः कुयोगिनाम् । निरस्तसाम्यातिशयेन राधसा । स्वधामनि ब्रह्मणि रंस्यते नमः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: namo namaste'stvṛṣabhāya sātvatāṃvidūrakāṣṭhāya muhuḥ kuyogināmnirastasāmyātiśayena rādhasāsvadhāmani brahmaṇi raṃsyate namaḥ
translation: I bow again and again to the protector of the Sāttvatas (or his devotees) who is beyond the reach of devotionless persons. Salutations to him, who being by nature unequalled and insurmountable, enjoys himself in his natural condition.

devanagari: यत्कीर्तनं यत्स्मरणं यदीक्षणं । यद् वंदनं यच्छ्रवणं यदर्हणम् । लोकस्य सद्यो विधुनोति कल्मषं । तस्मै सुभद्रश्रवसे नमो नमः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yatkīrtanaṃ yatsmaraṇaṃ yadīkṣaṇaṃyad vaṃdanaṃ yacchravaṇaṃ yadarhaṇamlokasya sadyo vidhunoti kalmaṣaṃtasmai subhadraśravase namo namaḥ
translation: Salutation to him of auspicious fame; (as) to praise him, to remember him, to look at him, to bow to him, to hear about him and to adore him, immediately purifies sins of men.

devanagari: विचक्षणा यच्चरणोपसादनात् । सङ्गं व्युदस्योभयतोऽन्तरात्मनः । विन्दन्ति हि ब्रह्मगतिं गतक्लमाः । तस्मै सुभद्रश्रवसे नमो नमः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: vicakṣaṇā yaccaraṇopasādanātsaṅgaṃ vyudasyobhayato'ntarātmanaḥvindanti hi brahmagatiṃ gataklamāḥtasmai subhadraśravase namo namaḥ
translation: I bow again and again to him of auspicious glory, by resorting to whose feet, the wise men shed off their heart’s attachment here and hereafter, and without any trouble, attain to the state of Brahman.

devanagari: तपस्विनो दानपरा यशस्विनो । मनस्विनो मंत्रविदः सुमङ्गलाः । क्षेमं न विन्दन्ति विना यदर्पणं । तस्मै सुभद्रश्रवसे नमो नमः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tapasvino dānaparā yaśasvinomanasvino maṃtravidaḥ sumaṅgalāḥkṣemaṃ na vindanti vinā yadarpaṇaṃtasmai subhadraśravase namo namaḥ
translation: I salute again and again to him, without offering whom (the fruit of one’s actions), none can attain happiness (or peace), even though they perform penance, or are very charitable or have attained (high) reputation, or practised Yoga, or are knower of very auspicious mantras, or are of pure conduct.

devanagari: किरातहूणान्ध्रपुलिन्दपुल्कशा । आभीरकङ्का यवनाः खसादयः । येऽन्ये च पापा यदपाश्रयाश्रयाः । शुध्यन्ति तस्मै प्रभविष्णवे नमः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: kirātahūṇāndhrapulindapulkaśāābhīrakaṅkā yavanāḥ khasādayaḥye'nye ca pāpā yadapāśrayāśrayāḥśudhyanti tasmai prabhaviṣṇave namaḥ
translation:
[2] The Kirātas, the Hūṇas, the Āndhras, Pulindas, Pulkasas, Ābhīras, Kakas (Śakas), Yavanas, Khasas and other (sinful) tribes and other sinners are purged of their sins even by taking refuge in those who depend on him. To that Almighty Lord, we offer our greetings.
19[3]. May the Divine Lord be propitious unto me—the Lord who is (to be meditated as) the Soul, by the knowers of the Soul,[4] as the Supreme God, by the devotees, as the three Vedas incarnate[5] by the followers of Karma kāṇḍa, as the veritable dharma[6] by the followers of dharma (religion), as the goal (to be achieved), by performers of penance,[7] the Lord, whose form is observed with great amazement by sincere worshippers like gods Brahmā, Śaṅkara etc.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
For the role of Bhagavatism in the social absorption of foreigners vide R.K. Mukerjee’s History of Indian Civilisation Vol. I., pp. 230-249.
This shows the attempts of proselytisation on the part of the Vaiṣṇavas. The following is the information in brief about tribes mentioned here,
Kirātas—a Himalayan tribe in the eastern region.
Hūṇas—The famous invading tribes from central Asia who established their dynasties in India.
Āndhras—According to the Aitareya Brāhmaṇa, a non-Aryan tribe living on the southern fringe of Āryāvarta. Formerly they were in the Vindhya region. Their settlement to the south in the present Andhra Pradesh is a later development.
Pulindas—An aboriginal tribe mentioned along with the Āndhras in the Aitareya Brāhmaṇa. They inhabited the Vindhya region upto 600 A.D.
Pulkasas or Pukkasa in Manu—An outcaste people.
Ābhīras—A foreign people who entered India at about the Śaka invasion and went on migrating from the Punjab till they settled in northwest Deccan. They are reported to have defeated Arjuna and carried away Yādava women.
Kaṅkas—Śakas. The original home of these people was in the valley of the Oxus and Jaxartes.
Yavanas—The Indo-Greeks who ruled in the north-western part of India.
Khasas—Identified with modern Khakkas who live in Kashmir.

Footnote [3]:
Padaratnāvalī interprets: May the Supreme Soul be gracious unto me—Soul who is the Supreme Lord to those who have directly realized Brahman (and have attained liberation), as the Lord is favourable to the followers of the Veda, dharma (path of duties prescribed by scripture) and penance and whose characteristics (e.g. creation of the world etc.) have been inferred by sincere devotees like Brahmā, Śiva etc.

Footnote [4]:
ātmavatām—(i) Steadfast in pure Yoga—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) Those who have directly realized Brahman or attained liberation—Padaratnāvalī
(iii) Those who have realized the non-ḍifference from Brahman—Bālaprabodhini

Footnote [5]:
trayī-maya [mayaḥ]—(i) follower of the householder’s duties, e.g. agnihotra prescribed in the Vedas—Siddhāntapradīpa
(ii) the students of Veda to worship it in the form of three Vedas—Bālaprabodhini

Footnote [6]:
dharma-mitya [mityaḥ]—(propitiated) by followers of the duties of celibates—Siddhāntapradīpa
(ii) performers of sacrifices should worship him as dharma—Bālaprabodhini

Footnote [7]:
tapo-maya [mayaḥ]—(worshipped) by the followers of vānaprastha and sannyāsa āśramas—Siddhāntapradīpa
Performers of penance should propitiate him as Tapas.

devanagari: स एष आत्मात्मवतामधीश्वरः । त्रयीमयो धर्ममयस्तपोमयः । गतव्यलीकैरजशङ्करादिभिः । वितर्क्यलिङ्गो भगवान्प्रसीदताम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eṣa ātmātmavatāmadhīśvaraḥtrayīmayo dharmamayastapomayaḥgatavyalīkairajaśaṅkarādibhiḥvitarkyaliṅgo bhagavānprasīdatām

devanagari: श्रियः पतिर्यज्ञपतिः प्रजापतिः । धियां पतिर्लोकपतिर्धरापतिः । पतिर्गतिश्चान्धकवृष्णिसात्वतां । प्रसीदतां मे भगवान् सतां पतिः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śriyaḥ patiryajñapatiḥ prajāpatiḥdhiyāṃ patirlokapatirdharāpatiḥpatirgatiścāndhakavṛṣṇisātvatāṃprasīdatāṃ me bhagavān satāṃ patiḥ
translation: May the Lord—protector of the good, be gracious unto me—the Lord, who is the master of the goddess of Wealth, the Lord of Sacrifices of all beings, the controller of mental or intellectual faculties, the protector of the worlds, the Lord of the earth, the Lord and protector of Andhaka, Vṛṣṇi and Śāttvata clans.

devanagari: यदङ्घ्र्यभिध्यानसमाधिधौतया । धियानुपश्यन्ति हि तत्त्वमात्मनः । वदन्ति चैतत् कवयो यथारुचं । स मे मुकुंदो भगवान् प्रसीदताम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yadaṅghryabhidhyānasamādhidhautayādhiyānupaśyanti hi tattvamātmanaḥvadanti caitat kavayo yathārucaṃsa me mukuṃdo bhagavān prasīdatām
translation: May Lord Mukunda (Kṛṣṇa) be propitious unto me by constant meditation of whose [Kṛṣṇa’s] feet, the wise purify their intellect and realize the true nature of the soul, and whom the wise describe as attributeful or attributeless (according to their taste or capacity).

devanagari: प्रचोदिता येन पुरा सरस्वती । वितन्वताऽजस्य सतीं स्मृतिं हृदि । स्वलक्षणा प्रादुरभूत् किलास्यतः । स मे ऋषीणां ऋषभः प्रसीदताम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: pracoditā yena purā sarasvatīvitanvatā'jasya satīṃ smṛtiṃ hṛdisvalakṣaṇā prādurabhūt kilāsyataḥsa me ṛṣīṇāṃ ṛṣabhaḥ prasīdatām
translation: May the most Excellent Sage be gracious to me—the sage who formerly (at the beginning of the creation) extended, i.e. awakened, the memory regarding the (procedure of) creation (of the universe) in the heart of Brahmā, and who directed the goddess Sarasvatī (the verbal form of the Veda) along with its characteristic supplements (e.g. śikṣā, vyākaraṇa etc.) to issue from the mouth of Brahmā.

devanagari: भूतैर्महद्भिर्य इमाः पुरो विभुः । निर्माय शेते यदमूषु पूरुषः । भुङ्क्ते गुणान् षोडश षोडशात्मकः । सोऽलङ्कृषीष्ट भगवान् वचांसि मे ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūtairmahadbhirya imāḥ puro vibhuḥnirmāya śete yadamūṣu pūruṣaḥbhuṅkte guṇān ṣoḍaśa ṣoḍaśātmakaḥso'laṅkṛṣīṣṭa bhagavān vacāṃsi me
translation: May the venerable Lord grace (beautify) my words—the Omnipresent Lord, having created these bodies with five gross elements (or with mahat and other elements) lies (dwells) within them as antaryāmin and becomes (even etymologically) the real Puruṣa—one who lies in a town, i.e. body). He illumines and protects the sixteen qualities (i.e. the constituents) of the body (viz. 11 sense organs and 5 elements), by inspiring with life these sixteen.[8]
purport:
Footnote [8]:
bhuṅkte......ṣoḍośātmakaḥ [ṣoḍośātmaka]—Bhāgavata Candrikā explains that the Lord possessing the sixteen constituents, viz. eleven sense organs and five elements, enjoys the sixteen ‘qualities’ viz. five objects of senses, e.g. sound, touch etc., five functions of organs, e.g. speech etc., five functions of Prāṇas or vital breaths and mind.
Subodhinī gives in detail how the Lord divides himself in different ‘enjoyer-enjoyed’ pairs (e.g. man—woman) and sports himself in the enjoyment of the world.

devanagari: नमस्तस्मै भगवते वासुदेवाय वेधसे । पपुर्ज्ञानमयं सौम्या यन्मुखाम्बुरुहासवम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: namastasmai bhagavate vāsudevāya vedhasepapurjñānamayaṃ saumyā yanmukhāmburuhāsavam
translation: I bow to the venerable Vyāsa,[9] the learned author of the Mahābhārata etc., from whose lotus-like mouth his gentle disciples drank the honey in the form of knowledge.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
Here the reading in the Nirnaya Sagara edition (Bombay 1905) is followed. The text before Padaratnāvalī reads Vāsudevāya vedhase—Padaratnāvalī interprets this differently:
(i) Salute to the Lord Vāsudeva from whose lotus-like mouth Brahmā and others, who deserved to drink the Soma-juice of knowledge, drank the honey of knowledge.
Thus taking saumya as (i) Uddhava and others, (ii) Nārada and others (iii) Vyāsa and others, (iv) Vaiśampāyana anḍ others, the salutes are offered to Kṛṣṇa, god Brahmā, Nārada and Vyāsa respectively.

devanagari: एतद् एवात्मभू राजन् नारदाय विपृच्छते । वेदगर्भोऽभ्यधात् साक्षाद् यदाह हरिरात्मनः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: etad evātmabhū rājan nāradāya vipṛcchatevedagarbho'bhyadhāt sākṣād yadāha harirātmanaḥ
translation:
Oh King, the Self-born God (Brahmā), the source of the Vedas, explained this to the enquiring Nārada, which was directly narrated to him (Brahmā) by Hari.
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां संहितायां । द्वितीयस्कंधे चतुर्थोऽध्यायः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmadbhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃdvitīyaskaṃdhe caturtho'dhyāyaḥ
